fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Hampshire Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Hampshire - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity new hampshire, how to transfer physical therapist license to new hampshire, physical therapist compact new hampshire, new hampshire physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: New Hampshire OPLC — Physical Therapy
board.parent_dept: New Hampshire Office of Professional Licensure and Certification (OPLC)

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in New Hampshire.

steps.title: Create an OPLC Online Licensing Portal Account
steps.description: Visit the OPLC Online Licensing Portal and complete the registration process for a new user account if you do not already have one.

steps.title: Start a New Physical Therapist Application
steps.description: Log into your OPLC Online Licensing Portal account, navigate to the 'Apply for a New License' section, and select the Physical Therapist application.

steps.title: Complete Online Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form, upload necessary initial documents, and pay the $100 application fee through the portal.

steps.title: Submit Required Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your physical therapy program, NPTE scores, and verifications of licensure from any other states where you hold or have held a license, to be sent directly to the New Hampshire Office of Professional Licensure and Certification.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Approval
steps.description: The New Hampshire Physical Therapy Governing Board will review your complete application and all supporting documents. You can monitor the status of your application via your OPLC Online Licensing Portal account until approval and license issuance.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a New Hampshire physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does New Hampshire have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. New Hampshire participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in New Hampshire without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to New Hampshire?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Hampshire while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in New Hampshire
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in New Hampshire, create an account and submit your application through the OPLC Online Licensing Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the OPLC Online Licensing Portal and click 'New Users Register Here'. Follow the prompts to create your username and password, then verify your email address to complete registration.
